title: Linuxcare, Inc. -- Enterprise Service and Support for All Things Linux
author: Peter S. Kastner / Aberdeen Group
abstract: Aberdeen Group profiles Linuxcare, Inc., a San Francisco-based specialist in enterprise Linux service and support founded in 1998. The report evaluates Linuxcare's four service pillars -- technical support, professional services, education/training, and benchmarking/certification -- and its partnerships with Dell, IBM, Sun, and TurboLinux. Aberdeen concludes Linuxcare has proven viability as a Linux expertise source but faces challenges scaling headcount and quality assurance.
importance_rationale: First detailed Aberdeen profile of a pure-play Linux services firm; captures the nascent commercial Linux support ecosystem at a critical inflection point in 1999.
relevance_rationale: Linux enterprise support market has matured considerably; Linuxcare's approach foreshadowed Red Hat support and Canonical enterprise services models.
prescience_rationale: Aberdeen correctly identified Linux adoption acceleration and need for professional services; Linuxcare itself did not survive as an independent company (acquired/restructured by 2001).
